complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Harden Electron startup and packaging for Windows and macOS
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down into subtasks for: 1) Electron startup optimizations (preload, contextIsolation, lazy loading), 2) Windows NSIS packaging config, 3) macOS dmg/pkg packaging config, 4) electron-updater integration, 5) Cross-platform testing and benchmarking per test strategy.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Involves platform-specific Electron configurations, performance profiling, multiple optimizations (preload scripts, security settings), packaging for two OS, auto-updater, and rigorous testing across environments. Cross-platform nature and Electron v28+ best practices increase complexity, but well-documented[1][2].

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement code signing for macOS and Windows production builds
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Create subtasks for: 1) macOS Developer ID cert setup and entitlements.plist, 2) macOS notarization with apple API, 3) Windows EV cert with signtool integration, 4) Secure CI/CD cert storage in GitHub Actions and verification tests.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Requires handling platform-specific certs, notarization APIs, entitlements, secure CI/CD secrets management, and verification tools (spctl, signtool). High complexity due to external dependencies (certs, Apple APIs) and distribution requirements.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add crash reporting and product analytics integration
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Sentry Electron integration with release tracking, 2) PostHog analytics event tracking for key flows, 3) Opt-in consent UI and privacy compliance testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Standard SDK integrations with good docs, but involves privacy-sensitive opt-in UI, event tracking across app flows, and crash simulation testing. Moderate complexity with cross-process Electron considerations.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create first-run onboarding flow for credentials and local model setup
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Subtasks: 1) SvelteKit wizard UI components (welcome, API keys, model download, project create), 2) Cross-platform credential encryption with keytar, 3) Local model download/error handling for Ollama/LM Studio, 4) E2E testing of full flow.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Multi-step Svelte UI wizard, external service integrations (API keys, model downloads), encryption, error recovery, and comprehensive E2E tests. UI complexity and external dependencies elevate it beyond simple forms.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Improve local project management, memory persistence, and WebSocket reliability
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) SQLite schema/CRUD for projects with FTS5 indexing, 2) better-sqlite3 Python backend integration, 3) WebSocket reconnection with exponential backoff, 4) Performance load testing (1000+ entries), 5) Network simulation tests.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Combines database design (SQLite FTS5), Python backend changes, real-time WebSocket reliability with reconnection logic, performance optimization, and load testing. Multiple technical domains increase complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement memory capture, search, and retrieval system
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Subtasks covering: 1) Vector embeddings pipeline with sentence-transformers, 2) SQLite vector extension or LanceDB setup, 3) Hybrid BM25+cosine search implementation, 4) Auto-capture logic for chat/context, 5) Performance benchmarking (<200ms), 6) Accuracy testing (>85%).
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Advanced ML features: vector embeddings, hybrid semantic/keyword search, new storage backend choice, auto-capture logic, strict perf/accuracy metrics. High technical risk and implementation effort.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Expand agent orchestration and background task execution
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Queue system (BullMQ/SQLite) setup, 2) Agent registry and LLM routing logic, 3) Worker pools for parallel execution, 4) WebSocket real-time status updates, 5) Concurrency testing (10 tasks), 6) Error recovery mechanisms.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Builds distributed task queue system with concurrency, agent routing, real-time monitoring, and error handling. Complex state management, race conditions, and integration with existing WebSocket/memory systems.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add organizations, team workspaces, and cloud account system
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Subtasks: 1) Supabase auth setup (email/OAuth), 2) Postgres schema for orgs/workspaces, 3) Row-level security policies, 4) API routes for CRUD/invites/permissions, 5) Local SQLite to cloud migration, 6) Multi-tenant testing, 7) UI components.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Full SaaS backend: auth, database design, RLS security, APIs, migration strategy, multi-tenancy. High complexity from security model, data migration, and scaling considerations.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement cloud sync for projects, memories, and agent state
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down: 1) CRDT/OT conflict resolution design, 2) Bi-directional sync queues with retries, 3) Offline-first architecture, 4) WebSocket real-time sync, 5) Multi-device consistency testing, 6) Network interruption simulations, 7) Data integrity validation, 8) Performance optimization.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Extremely complex: distributed systems, CRDTs/OT for conflicts, offline-first, real-time sync, multi-device testing. High risk of data corruption; requires deep systems expertise.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add GitHub/GitLab integration for repos and pull requests
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Subtasks: 1) OAuth2 flows for GitHub/GitLab, 2) Local repo cloning and management, 3) PR diff parsing with LLM analysis, 4) Context injection into agent prompts and E2E testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Multiple OAuth providers, API integrations, local git ops, LLM processing of diffs, prompt engineering. Moderate-high complexity from external APIs and git interactions.
